meaning: n.龙骨(船的脊骨),平底船vt.装以龙骨,使倾覆vi.倾覆
examples: The keel hit the rock first.
Jason had helped him out with a series of loans, until he could get back on an even keel.
It was built by Portsmouth Dockyard, and its keel laid in January 1912.
Mr Keel said he was looking at new ways of working to make savings.
These have two hulls, spaced widely apart and connected by a raftlike structure and no large keel.
